projectDescription: A Telegram-bot-powered appointment booking system built on Next.js that lets end-users reserve sessions via chat and gives administrators a web dashboard to manage schedules, services, and reservations — backed by Vercel KV and optional Google Calendar integration.
keyFeatures: Conversational Telegram bot for end-to-end appointment booking
Multi-step booking flow: professional → service → name → date → time → confirm
Real-time availability checking with overlap prevention
Per-user rate limiting (20 messages / 60 s window)
Update deduplication to prevent double-processing Telegram webhooks
Admin web dashboard built with Next.js (reservations + configuration tabs)
Configurable professionals with per-day time slot schedules
Configurable services with name, duration (minutes), and price
Holiday management — block specific dates across all professionals
Secure admin authentication with bcrypt, session tokens, and brute-force protection (5 attempts, 15-minute lockout)
Vercel KV (Redis-compatible) for persistent storage in production; in-memory fallback for local development
Optional Google Calendar integration for availability checks and event creation
Admin password hashing utility via scripts/generate-hash.js
publicApiSurface: GET  /api              — health overview, lists webhook and healthcheck endpoints
GET  /api/healthcheck  — liveness probe; returns status, message, and timestamp
POST /api/webhook      — Telegram webhook receiver (messages + callback queries)
POST /api/admin/login  — authenticate with username/password; sets admin_session cookie
POST /api/admin/logout — invalidates session cookie; redirects to /login
DELETE /api/admin/reservations — delete a reservation by id (auth required)
GET  /api/admin/config — retrieve current bot configuration (auth required)
PUT  /api/admin/config — update professionals, services, and holidays (auth required)
